segments.text: Give ear to my prayer, O God, and hide not yourself from my plea.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Attend to me, and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and I moan,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: because of the noise of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And I say, 'Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest;'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: yes, I would wander far away; I would lodge in the wilderness. Selah
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I would hurry to find a shelter from the raging wind and tempest.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Destroy, O Lord, divide their tongues; for I see violence and strife in the city.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Day and night they go around it on its walls,
and iniquity and trouble are within it;
destructive forces are in its midst;
oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
For it is not an enemy who taunts me— then I could bear it; it is not an adversary who deals insolently with me— then I could hide from him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But it is you, a man, my equal, my companion, my familiar friend.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: mine equal: Heb. according to my rank

segments.text: We used to take sweet counsel together; within God's house we walked in the throng.

footnotes: We sweetened counsel: Heb. Who sweetened counsel

segments.text: Let death steal over them; let them go down to Sheol alive; for evil is in their dwelling place and in their heart.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But I call to God, and the LORD will save me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan, and he hears my voice.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: He redeems my soul in safety from the battle that I wage, for many are arrayed against me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: God will give ear and humble them, he who is enthroned from of old, Selah because they do not change and do not fear God.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: Because: or, With whom also there be no changes, yet they

segments.text: My companion stretched out his hand against his friends; he violated his covenant.
His speech was smooth as butter, yet war was in his heart; his words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
Cast your burden on the LORD, and he will sustain you; he will never permit the righteous to be moved.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But you, O God, will cast them down into the pit of destruction; men of blood and treachery shall not live out half their days. But I will trust in you.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: bloody: Heb. men of bloods and deceit
shall not half their days: Heb. shall not half their days
